note: Internal schema used to generate comments in the owner-only config.jsonc file.

distro.description: WSL distribution used on Windows; ignored on Linux and macOS.

default_accelerator.description: Default Colab hardware when create_session does not specify one.

default_runtime_version.description: Default Colab runtime image label. Latest is recommended.

default_high_ram.description: Whether new sessions request High-RAM by default. L4, G4, H100, v5e-1, and v6e-1 override false because Colab only offers them with High-RAM.

default_timeout_seconds.description: Default timeout for supported MCP operations.

compute_warning_minutes.description: Elapsed session time at which Codex reports a compute-use warning.

default_max_lifetime_minutes.description: Default hard session lifetime; 0 disables the plugin timer.

notification_mode.description: Desktop popup policy. Silent notification history is always retained.

max_concurrent_sessions.description: Maximum active Colab assignments allowed before create_session is blocked.

transfer_compression.description: Default compression for managed uploads and downloads; folders are always archived.

transfer_parallelism.description: Default number of simultaneous chunks for managed transfers.

retry_attempts.description: Total attempts for retry-safe read-only CLI operations and managed transfer chunks. Mutating operations are never automatically repeated.

default_drive_checkpoint_folder.description: Default folder inside MyDrive/codex-colab when a Drive save omits drive_path.
default_drive_checkpoint_folder.allowed: A relative folder path inside codex-colab; no absolute path, empty component, '.', or '..'

require_cost_acknowledgement.description: Ask for explicit user approval before every new Colab allocation. Set false only when the user grants standing authorization for session creation.

require_secret_enable_approval.description: Ask before exposing a locally stored credential to a Colab session. False lets the LLM enable only named Colab Remote aliases automatically; values remain hidden.

allowed_local_roots.description: Exact existing local folders the plugin may access for files and notebooks.
allowed_local_roots.allowed: Existing directories approved by the user
